tool: macOS sample(1) CPU sampler, 1ms interval, 12s window
tool_limitations: CPU-side sampling only. Cannot attribute time to specific Metal kernels (qmm_q3_K vs qmm_q4_K etc.) — needs Xcode Instruments Metal System Trace for the kernel-level breakdown.

workload.model: qwen3_dense / Qwen3.5-9B-MLX-4bit with model.rotated.safetensors (64 ffn_gate/ffn_up re-quantised to 3-bit affine after Hadamard rotation)

cpu_attribution_samples.interpretation: CPU is largely sitting in pthread_cond_wait inside mlx_eval — i.e. blocked on the GPU finishing the dispatched work. The slowdown is GPU-side, not CPU-side.

hot_phase: GPU execution of quantized matrix-vector kernel (qmv) at bits=3
outcome_explanation: The 3-bit rotated configuration is 1.43x slower than the 4-bit baseline despite touching ~25% fewer weight bytes per forward pass. The CPU sampler confirms the slowdown lives in GPU execution time (pthread_cond_wait dominates inside mlx_eval). MLX's qmv quantized matrix-vector function is the visible hot op; the specific Metal kernel (likely qmm_q3_K_K or equivalent) is slower per-byte than its 4-bit counterpart. This is precisely the kernel-level bottleneck that ADR 0020 Track K is designed to attack.

recommended_next_step.first_action: Run Metal System Trace (Xcode Instruments) on the same 3-bit rotated workload. Identify the exact Metal pipeline name corresponding to qmv at bits=3, measure its threadgroup occupancy + memory throughput against the 4-bit equivalent. Compare bytes-fetched-per-dispatch ratio.
recommended_next_step.expected_artifact: ax.metal_kernel_profile.v1 with: kernel_name, threads_per_threadgroup, registers_per_thread, memory_read_GBps, gpu_time_us_per_call, comparison_vs_4bit
recommended_next_step.decision_gate: If 3-bit qmv kernel uses < 50% of theoretical peak bandwidth AND has substantial dispatch overhead (small grid, low occupancy), a custom Metal kernel is the right Track-K target. If it already saturates bandwidth, the slowdown is fundamental and 3-bit-as-bandwidth-win is the wrong attack — switch focus to ADR 0022 D2 calibration improvements (input-aware quant) instead.
